sections.paragraph: ἅπαξ λεγόμ. Isa 3:24, prob. a large cloak, such as used to be made of a round form; comp. of פְּתִי i.q. Ch. פְּתַי breadth, and גִּיל round, or i.q. Ch. גֹּולָא a cloak.-LXX, χιτὼν μεσοπόρφυρος. Vulg. fascia pectoralis. Those who adopt the latter rendering regard the word as being compounded of Ch. פְּתָג linen, and פָּתִיל thread, cord; but this does not appear suitable. [In Thes. it is supposed to be compounded of “פְּתִיךְ and גִּיל i.e. a variegated garment for festive occasions.”]
